value.body:
## Problem

Ticket terminal cleanup can remove a managed implementation worktree shortly after the ticket moves to Done. Historical execution showed follow-up changes reported as uncommitted/unpublished before terminal cleanup removed the worktree.

Ticket status alone is not sufficient evidence that the implementation workspace is safe to delete.

## Desired behavior

Cycle must never automatically remove an implementation worktree while it contains dirty files, commits not protected by a branch, commits not pushed according to policy, or a handover newer than the branch's recorded publication state.

## Implementation detail

- Add an authoritative cleanup preflight in the git-worktrees package and call it from terminal-ticket cleanup.
- Preflight must inspect:
  - active leases and non-terminal tasks
  - tracked, staged, and untracked changes
  - worktree HEAD versus last handed-over SHA
  - branch association head versus worktree/finalizer head
  - local commits not protected by a durable branch
  - required remote publication and pushed SHA/ref
  - path containment and managed-worktree identity
- `cleanupTerminalTicket` must fail closed when any finding is unsafe.
- When the ticket becomes terminal while work remains:
  - do not remove the worktree;
  - transition the implementation context to a visible `cleanup-blocked` or `recovery-required` state;
  - record normalized blocking reasons;
  - notify the user with actions to resume implementation, retry handover/push, or explicitly discard.
- Where safe and policy permits, create a deterministic backup branch before any forced cleanup.
- Forced discard must require an explicit human action, current preflight token/version, and confirmation listing exactly what will be lost.
- Re-run preflight under the cleanup lease immediately before removal; UI-cached state cannot authorize deletion.
- Persist lifecycle events for preflight, refusal, backup, removal, and recovery.
- Reconciliation after a process restart must preserve dirty/unpublished work and converge without false `removed` status.
- Keep this automatic safety invariant separate from the broader Tasks/Worktrees administration UI in `UKN-1VY75`.

## Acceptance criteria

- Moving a ticket to Done cannot remove a dirty or unpublished implementation worktree.
- Clean, fully handed-over and pushed worktrees remain eligible for normal terminal cleanup.
- Unsafe cleanup produces actionable structured reasons rather than being silently skipped.
- Forced cleanup cannot proceed without a successful backup when backup policy requires one.
- Cleanup decisions are lease/fencing protected and revalidated immediately before mutation.
- Removed records retain audit history and recovery branch details.
- Repeated terminal transitions and cleanup requests are idempotent.

## Tests

- Dirty tracked, staged, untracked, and deleted-file cases.
- Detached HEAD with no protecting branch.
- Local branch published but required remote SHA missing.
- Successful handover/push followed by safe cleanup.
- Concurrent follow-up task racing with Done transition.
- Process restart between backup, Git removal, filesystem removal, and record transition.
- Symlink/path-containment and stale-preflight failure tests.

## Evidence

The `UKN-WB555` managed worktree was removed by terminal cleanup after follow-up execution had reported seven intended files as modified and no successful follow-up handover was recorded.

frontmatter.title: Block terminal ticket cleanup when implementation work is dirty or unpublished

value.title: Block terminal ticket cleanup when implementation work is dirty or unpublished
